text.en: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Charizard.
During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Tangrowth by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Tangrowth. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Tangrowth's, this attack does 120 damage instead.

flavor_text.en: Its arms are made of plants that bind themselves to things. They grow back right away if cut.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.
If Toxicroak has any Psychic Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If Toxicroak has any Fighting Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavor_text.en: It has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for more potency.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Zapdos .
You may discard all Lightning Energy attached to Zapdos . If you do, this attack's base damage is 80 instead of 40.

flavor_text.en: A Pokémon that roamed the skies in the dinosaur era. Its teeth are like saw blades.

flavor_text.en: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavor_text.en: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun's rays with its whole body.

flavor_text.en: The leer that floats in darkness belongs to a Gengar delighting in casting curses on people.

flavor_text.en: It prevents prey from escaping by instantaneously freezing moisture in the air.

flavor_text.en: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.

flavor_text.en: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

flavor_text.en: The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavor_text.en: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

flavor_text.en: It whittles its constantly growing fangs by gnawing on hard things. It can chew apart cinder walls.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavor_text.en: When evolving, its body takes in surrounding materials. As a result, there are many body variations.

flavor_text.en: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

flavor_text.en: While it loves floral honey, it won't gather any itself. Instead, it plots to steal some from Combee.
The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

flavor_text.en: It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

It soars high in the sky, riding on updrafts like a glider. It carries food tucked in its bill.

rules_text.en: Search your deck for Arceus, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Attach Bench Shield to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Attach Buffer Piece to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put a basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand. If you don't have that many basic Energy cards in your discard pile, put all of them into your hand.

Attach Expert Belt to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If
that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Attach Lucky Egg to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If
that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Play Old Amber as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Old Amber counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Old Amber is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Old Amber can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Old Amber from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

text.en: Put damage counters on the Defending Pokémon until the Defending Pokémon has the same remaining HP as Swalot. (If the Defending Pokémon has the same or less remaining HP as Swalot, this attack does nothing.)
You may do 50 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon, and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

flavor_text.en: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

rules_text.en: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Play Dome Fossil as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Dome Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Dome Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Dome Fossil can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Dome Fossil from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

Play Helix Fossil as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Helix Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Helix Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Helix Fossil can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Helix Fossil from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

Search your discard pile for as many Grass Energy cards as you like and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.
